quality-gates.description: The 5-gate ladder. Every change passes these gates in order. A failure at any gate blocks promotion to the next. Read the project constitution before evaluating a gate — its articles (especially Article VI — Anti-Overengineering) define the pass/fail rubric.

gate.name: static-integrity
gate.when: On every save and pre-commit. Cheapest signal first.

checks.check: Lint passes with zero new warnings.
Format matches the project formatter.
Type check passes for typed languages.
Dead code / unused imports flagged and removed.
Dependency hygiene: no version drift, no unused deps added.

commands.default: npm run lint && npm run typecheck
commands.ruby: bundle exec rubocop && bundle exec srb tc
commands.typescript: npm run lint && npm run typecheck

gate.fail: Any error or new warning blocks the gate.

gate.name: contract-compliance
gate.when: Before merge, against the spec/plan/task contract.

checks.check: Public API matches the documented contract in spec.md / plan.md.
YAGNI: no speculative parameters, options, or hooks not in the spec.
DRY: no extraction below the third instance of duplication.
KISS: of the working approaches, the simplest one was chosen.
Anti-Speculation: no fallbacks for impossible scenarios.
output_schema sections from skill frontmatter all present in artifacts.
No undeclared side effects (filesystem, network, env mutation).

gate.pass: Contract diff is empty. Reviewer cites Articles IV, V, VI as satisfied.
gate.fail: Missing or extra sections, contract drift, premature abstraction, or speculative code.

gate.name: behavioral-validation

checks.check: Unit tests pass.
Integration tests pass.
Each acceptance criterion in spec.md has at least one passing test.
For bugfixes: a regression test exists that fails on the old code.
Test-first evidence: tests authored before or with production code (Article II).
Coverage thresholds met (project-defined).

gate.pass: Full suite green. New behavior covered. Coverage thresholds met.
gate.fail: Any failing test, any uncovered new branch, missing regression test.

gate.name: pattern-consistency

checks.check: Code uses existing helpers, not parallel re-implementations.
Naming follows project conventions (CamelCase, file-name style, etc.).
Error handling matches the project pattern (typed errors, wrap chains, etc.).
Standards in specs/standards/ are respected; novel patterns require an ADR.
File and function size within Article VI limits (≤ 300 lines / ≤ 30 lines).

gate.pass: No out-of-pattern code without an ADR. No standards violations.
gate.fail: Novel pattern introduced without justification, or standards violation.

gate.name: observability-readiness

checks.check: Logging covers happy path and failure modes.
Metrics exposed for the change's success/failure rate where applicable.
Errors are reported with enough context to debug from production logs.
Rollback plan documented in PR description for risky changes.

gate.pass: Operator can detect and roll back the change without reading source.
gate.fail: Silent failure modes, no metrics, or no rollback path for risky changes.

coverage.require-new-tests: For new features and bugfixes (Article II).
